rulings.Case Title: MONICA HUJAZI, AN INDIVIDUIAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE VS. SYDNEY A. LUSCUTOFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Defendant Sidney A. Luscutoff And Luscutoff, Lendormy And Associates For Terminating Sanctions For Disobedience Of Discovery Orders
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANTS SYDNEY LUSCUTOFF, LUSCUTOFF, LENDORMY & ASSOCIATES' Motion Of Defendant Sidney A. Luscutoff And Luscutoff, Lendormy And Associates For Terminating Sanctions For Disobedience Of Discovery Orders Pro Tem Judge Stephen Zollman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Pursuant to the agreement of the parties, the motion is continued to May 9, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to szollman1@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.

rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, April 30, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint. As defendants concede, motions for leave to file an amended complaint are "viewed with great liberality." It appears the parties may have already agreed to a shortened schedule for defendants to bring summary adjudication motions; if not, plaintiff indicates his willingness to do so. The pending motion is therefore GRANTED. Any motion to continue the August 8 trial date is to be filed in Department 206. rulings.Case Title: SCOTT D. AYERS ET AL VS. HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, April 30, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFFs SCOTT AYERS AND ILONA MARSOVSZKI'S Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expenses. (Part 1 of 2) If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Plaintiffs Scott Ayers and Ilona Marsovszki's motion for attorneys' fees, costs, and expenses is granted in part. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Plaintiffs are awarded $40,059 in attorney's fees. The portion of plaintiffs' motion seeking costs and expenses has been addressed in the ruling on defendant Hyundai Motor America's motion to strike or tax costs and will not be repeated here. While it is undoubtedly true that counsel for plaintiffs achieved an excellent result for their clients, it is equally true that obtaining an excellent result does not immunize a fees request from scrutiny. Hyundai has challenged all or portions of numerous entries on the billing records submitted by counsel for plaintiffs on this motion. The grounds for challenging those entries are: 1) the claimed hourly rates of plaintiffs' counsel are too high; 2) many of the descriptions of the work performed are vague or cryptic such that a reader cannot determine what work was done; 3) some of the tasks performed by plaintiffs' counsel should not have taken as long as claimed, particularly since the work involved "stock" matters in repetitive litigation; 4) a name partner should not have reviewed routine work performed by others in his office; 5) duplicative work was performed by some of the nine attorneys who worked on the case for some of the entries; 6) some of the work performed by attorneys were "paralegal tasks" that should be billed at a lower hourly rate; 7) the Altman firm's recording of its time in .25 of an hour increments rather than .10 of an hour increments resulted in overbilling; and 8) plaintiffs' counsel engaged in improper "value billing." Having had no prior dealings with this case and thus not being able to observe firsthand anything about this case other than this motion and the costs motion, the court is at a disadvantage in evaluating Hyundai's many arguments why the fees sought by plaintiffs ($51,332.50- $1950 (for the withdrawn 3/17/17 entry by Mr. Altman) x a multiplier of 1.5) should be reduced. Nonetheless, the court is required to rule on this motion and is limited in doing so to the materials submitted by the parties on this motion and the court's own extensive experience with fees motion. The claimed hourly rates by the two lead attorneys for plaintiffs are in line with other plaintiffs' "lemon law" counsel and are reasonable. The claimed hourly rates for the other attorneys who worked on the case are approximately one-third higher than what the court believes is in line with rates for similar counsel doing similar work and, based on that finding, the lodestar amount is reduced by $10,000. (tentative ruling continued in Part 2 of 2)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT D. AYERS ET AL VS. HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Scott Ayers, Ilona Marsovszki Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expenses (Added For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only)
rulings.Rulings: Plaintiff Scott Ayers, Ilona Marsovszki Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expenses (Added For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only) (Part 2 of 2) The arguments that a name partner should not have performed review work, duplicative work was performed, and plaintiffs' counsel engaged in improper value billing are rejected as not being supported by the record on this motion. However, the record on this motion shows that there is merit to the arguments that some of the billing entries are too vague or cryptic, the length of time recorded for certain entries is excessive, some of the work performed by attorneys were paralegal tasks, and the Altman firm's minimum increment of .25 of an hour resulted in a larger fee amount than justified. Based on these findings, the lodestar amount is further reduced by $6000. Thus, the court calculates the lodestar amount as $33,382.50. Because of the excellent result achieved, the court applies a multiplier of 1.2. No greater multiplier is warranted in this relatively simple case due to the lack of any showing that this case prevented plaintiffs' counsel from taking on any other work. The failure to award any multiplier would improperly ignore the approximately 2.75 more in monetary recovery than plaintiffs' cost of purchasing the vehicle. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT D. AYERS ET AL VS. HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike/Tax Costs; Decl. Of Jimmy Y. Park; Exhibit A
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, April 30, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA'S Motion To Strike/Tax Costs. If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Defendant Hyundai Motor America's motion to strike or tax costs is granted in part. $960 of the $4916.37 in claimed costs are taxed, resulting in $3956.37 in costs recoverable by plaintiffs Scott Ayers and Ilona Marsovszki against Hyundai. Hyundai's argument that the recoverability of plaintiffs' claimed costs should be determined by the standards in the general costs statute (CCP 1033.5) rather than the costs provision of the Song-Beverly Act (Civil Code 1794(d)) because plaintiffs filed a costs memorandum lacks merit. No case holds that by filing a costs memorandum a prevailing Song-Beverly plaintiff forfeits or waives his right to seek costs under the more generous standard in section 1794(d). Hyundai's argument puts form over substance and is contrary to the remedial purposes of the Song-Beverly Act. Thus, the portions of Hyundai's motion seeking to strike or tax costs based on non-recoverability of those costs per section 1033.5(b) are denied. $830 for claimed service of process costs is taxed because plaintiffs have not shown that the costs for serving the seven deposition subpoenas were reasonably incurred given that the depositions never occurred. Plaintiffs have shown that the expert fees of Thomas Lepper were reasonably incurred. $130 for claimed appearance attorneys is taxed because those appearances were only necessitated by plaintiffs' delay in filing their fees motion. Plaintiffs have shown that all other claimed "other" expenses - attorney services and messengers for court filings and service, travel, postage, and copies - were reasonably incurred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA ISABEL DELGADO VS. PRIMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, April 30, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT AMERICAN INTERNATIONAL GROUP, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Specially appearing defendant American International Group, Inc.'s motion to quash service for lack of personal jurisdiction is GRANTED. A plaintiff has the burden to demonstrate, by evidence, facts justifying the exercise of jurisdiction. (Vons Companies, Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434, 449.) Here, plaintiff relies on her complaint's allegations, not evidence. AIG's filing of a motion to dismiss on jurisdictional grounds in federal court was not a waiver. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MARIA ISABEL DELGADO VS. PRIMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, April 30, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANTS PRIMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY AND PRIMERICA FINANCIAL SERVICES INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Primerica Life Insurance Company's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is OVERRULED. The disputes over insurance policies all relate to Rameriz Alvarez Conejo's death, so joinder is proper, and it would be more efficient to conduct discovery jointly. Primerica raises fair points about possible prejudice and jury confusion if the case gets to trial, but those can be addressed with a severance motion in the trial court. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS SAAVEDRA VS. MACKIE BERTRAND MCDUFFY III ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answer To Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories And Requet For Production Of Documents; And To Compel Claimants Deposition And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: RESPONDENT GEICO INDEMNITY COMPANY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answer To Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories And Requet For Production Of Documents; And To Compel Claimants Deposition And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Stephen Zollman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in whole as to the motions to compel. As to the motion for sanctions, counsel is to confer with the clerk to make sure they have the last known contact information for Petitioner. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to szollman1@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE LAPAYOWKER VS. AIRBNB,INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Carlos Del Olmos'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue To Los Angeles County; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Carlos Del Olmo; Declaration Of Angel Alvarez; Declaration Of Christina Vanarelli (Motions)
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, April 30, 2018, Line 14, DEFENDANT CARLOS DEL OLMO'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue To Los Angeles County. The motion to change venue is DENIED. Venue is determined at the action's outset from the original complaint, even though, as here, a defendant that confers venue is later dismissed. (See Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) §3:489.) As to convenience, only the convenience of nonparty witnesses is important, and the nonparty witnesses here reside in Florida and Texas; i.e., a San Francisco venue would essentially be as convenient as Los Angeles. (See id. at §3:555.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ANNIE OKEEFE VS. KATHLEEN MCDONAGH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, April 30, 2018, Line 16, DEFENDANT KATHLEEN MCDONAGH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Kathleen McDonagh's demurrer to Annie O'Keefe's complaint (filed in January 2018) is SUSTAINED on the statute-of-limitations ground. The one-year limitations period of CCP 366.2 applies in all of the factual/legal scenarios posited. First, it applies if Ms. McDonagh is viewed as a surviving spouse after her husband's death in August 2014 in a community property scenario. (Dawes v. Rich (1997) 60 Cal.App.4th 24, 34.) Second, it applies if Ms. McDonagh is viewed as a trustee. (Wagner v. Wagner (2008) 162 Cal.App.4th 249, 255; Levine v. Levine (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 1256, 1265 (CCP 366.2 applies where no proceeding to administer decedent's estate and no notice to creditors).) Third, even were the complaint construed to establish Ms. McDonagh's acknowledgment of a debt in May 2015, the 366.2 statute ran in May 2016. All of this was before the petition for order confirming trust assets under Probate Code 17200 and 850 in September 2017 - and, again, before Ms. O'Keefe filed her complaint. Because Ms. O'Keefe has not shown how amendment could possibly cure her complaint's defect, the demurrer is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for McDonagh is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
